name: Riverfront Coffee Co.
short_fact: A cozy cafe with large windows overlooking the Chicago River, famous for their single-origin espresso blends.

name: Cloud Gate (The Bean)
short_fact: Anish Kapoor's iconic stainless steel sculpture reflects Chicago's skyline and has become a symbol of modern architecture.

name: Art Institute of Chicago
short_fact: Founded in 1879, this world-renowned museum houses over 300,000 artworks spanning from ancient times to contemporary.

name: Grant Park
short_fact: A 319-acre urban park along Lake Michigan featuring sculptures, gardens, and stunning views of the city skyline.

name: Millennium Park
short_fact: A 24.5-acre public park featuring the AT&T Plaza, Crown Fountain, and Jay Pritzker Pavilion with free concerts.

name: Chicago History Museum
short_fact: Established in 1856, this museum preserves Chicago's rich history from frontier settlement to modern times.

name: Lacuna Lofts - Street Art District
short_fact: A vibrant artist community featuring colorful murals, galleries, and street art installations throughout the neighborhood.

name: Navy Pier
short_fact: A historic pier extending into Lake Michigan with restaurants, shops, attractions, and a 148-foot tall Ferris wheel.

name: Chicago Cultural Center
short_fact: A 1897 Beaux-Arts landmark featuring stunning architecture, exhibitions, and free cultural programming.

name: Intelligentsia Coffee
short_fact: A specialty coffee roaster and cafe known for direct-trade coffees and artisanal pastries from local bakeries.

name: Museum of Science and Industry
short_fact: An interactive museum featuring the U-505 submarine, a Boeing 727, and hundreds of hands-on exhibits.

name: The Magnificent Mile
short_fact: A 13-block stretch of North Michigan Avenue lined with luxury boutiques, flagship stores, and world-class dining.
